name: @orrgal1/forge
description: PR forge chain — repo-agnostic. Forge hardwires no capability: it resolves every operation through /forge-setup config (per-repo forge.toml tooling + the machine-global capabilities.toml registry) and depends on being fully configured. Chain-blind PR ops (peer-reviewer ranking, and its siblings) are required capabilities defaulting to @orrgal1/devloop — strongly recommended; unconfigured required capability → NEEDS_SETUP, no built-in substitute. The restack capability is the one exception with a built-in git fallback (fetch base + merge into branch) when unwired; configurable via /forge-setup [restack] (e.g. @orrgal1/devloop's /restack, a command/instructions). /forge runs the arc end-to-end: start → goals (AWAIT_GOALS_REVIEW) → design (AWAIT_DESIGN_REVIEW) → scenarios (AWAIT_SCENARIOS_REVIEW) → tests → impl → proof-green → ci-green → review-green → ci-ready → READY (continuous ci-green stays armed until merge). Modes: auto (pause at goals + design + scenarios), manual (pause every phase), yolo (no contract pauses — drive to READY, stop only at genuine blockers; /forge-yolo is the thin wrapper); resume via /forge approve and /forge iterate "<feedback>". /forge-setup maps host-repo tooling (build/test/lint/typecheck/codegen/devenv/localenv/restack) into $FORGE_HOME; every skill resolves repo commands through it, so forge adopts into any repo. /forge-setup also wires recovery playbooks ([playbooks.<name>] in forge.toml): repo-scoped failure→recovery rules (when_output regex → then recovery command, with interactive + retry) forge consults automatically when a capability run fails — e.g. an ECR pull failing on an expired SSO token triggers `aws sso login` then retries the run — instead of blocking to ask the operator to auth; the fix-loops consult these playbooks on a failing capability run, and forge recognizes recurring unmapped failures cleared by a manual step and offers to capture a new playbook on the fly; recoveries run best-effort in every mode — an interactive one (browser auth) is attempted even under yolo/unattended and only becomes a genuine block if no human completes it. /forge-start opens the chain (brief → scaffold worktree → sentinel commit → draft PR), then hands off for a session switch (HANDOFF_WORKTREE — autopilot stops at the worktree boundary; re-run /forge inside the new worktree); idempotent re-run inside the worktree completes only what's missing. The PR body holds one tight non-collapsible brief on top, owned by /forge-brief (kept current as intent evolves — never left to stale), with each embedded chain artifact (/forge-proof, /forge-review) below it in its own idempotent collapsible block; each writer touches only its own region. /forge-goals captures intended end-state; /forge-scenarios drafts when:/then: scenarios (component-tier-observable); /forge-validations drafts checkable predicates for removal/structural goals with no runtime observable (a goal needs ≥1 proof — scenario or validation); /forge-tests writes/attaches a component-tier test per scenario; /forge-design maps each scenario to design elements. /forge-proof aggregates the attestation chain: goals loyal to source → covered by ≥1 proof → scenario attached to a real test → body matches when/then + AAA → linked tests pass → validations hold. Per-layer attestation: /forge-verify-{goals,scenarios,tests,match,runs,validations}. Fix-loops: /forge-impl-green (tests green), /forge-proof-green (proof PASS), /forge-ci-green (CI green — restacks every iteration, and runs --until-merge as a continuous background monitor after the first green that re-arms on every new HEAD until the PR merges, replacing any one-shot final CI step), /forge-review-green (0 open findings, every severity). External-block recognizer: when a halt is externally-resolvable (base behind/red, infra incident), the find_blocker capability (/find-blocker, @orrgal1/devloop) fans out across gh/Slack/infra to identify the peripheral blocker and emit a neutral condition spec, which forge maps to /forge-wait-for to watch that one condition (base PR CI, a Slack thread, any predicate command) and, once it clears, restacks and resumes the chain (mode-gated auto-resume in yolo/unattended), then self-terminates; genuine halts always stop. On READY, /forge arms /forge-review-watch so incoming peer feedback auto-dispatches to /forge-address-review, then the request_review capability (/request-review, @orrgal1/devloop) ranks the most relevant peer reviewer (same-stack reviewers > reviewers of the author's work > people the author reviews > code-area/CODEOWNERS); marking the PR ready-for-review + requesting the reviewer is a gated author gesture (AWAIT_REVIEW_REQUEST) requiring approval even in yolo. /forge-review is a thin chain wrapper over the `review` capability (/review, @orrgal1/devloop) that fans out review channels in parallel — lens-fanout + always-on /code-review and /security-review; custom via $FORGE_HOME/review-channels/ — to one ranked verdict; forge adds the proof gate, its three chain lenses, and the chain verdict ladder. /forge-map builds JSON domain snapshots (db/api/events/config via /forge-map-{db,api,events,config}, plus ad-hoc) under $FORGE_HOME/maps/main/ with lazy branch forks that absorb on merge. /forge-tool packages ad-hoc flows into reusable tools under $FORGE_HOME/tools/<name> (script / instructions / dir / agent forms). All repo state lives at $FORGE_HOME (default ~/.claude/forge/<repo-key>/) so worktrees share state. Per-PR artifacts live in-repo under $FORGE_ART/branches/<slug>/ ($FORGE_ART = .forge by default, or <prefix>/.forge via [artifacts].prefix); what of that metadata git tracks is configurable via [artifacts].track (default all; categories spec[goals+design]/proof[machine state]/loop/review/monitor — track=["spec"] keeps only the review surfaces), enforced by a generated $FORGE_ART/.gitignore scoped to branches/ — maps/tools live in $FORGE_HOME, outside that policy.
